title: Europe Trip Planned? Here's What You Need to Do Right Now

description:
Planning a Europe trip from India and feeling confused due to the current geopolitical situation? You’re not alone.

In this video, I’m sharing 6 practical, real-life tips based on my personal experience to help you plan smartly and avoid panic, especially when situations like war, airspace restrictions, or global uncertainty arise.

This is NOT a news update. This is a practical travel guide for Indian travellers so you don’t lose money and can travel with confidence.

In this video, I cover:

Why you should avoid panic decisions during uncertain situations
How to book refundable flights and stays for peace of mind
Why you should avoid jam-packed itineraries
What to check in your travel insurance policy (very important!)
What to do if you have already booked your flights, hotels or trains
My personal experience and approach during such travel situations

Whether you're travelling now or planning for the future, these tips will help you handle any geopolitical or travel disruption situation calmly and smartly.

👉 The goal is simple: protect your money, reduce stress, and travel with clarity, not fear.

00:00 - Is it safe to travel?
00:45 - Tip No.1
01:18 - Tip No. 2

chapters.title: What to do if you have already booked the trip?
What I do based on my travel experience

fulltitle: Europe Trip Planned? Here's What You Need to Do Right Now

comments.text:
f you’ve already booked your Europe trip, what are you planning to do right now?
👉 Go ahead / Wait / Cancel

Would love to know your situation, I’ll try to guide based on your case ❤
comments.author: @heenajshah

comments.text: bajaj travel ace insurance covers concellation due to war? my flight will be through sharjah and abu dhabi. if flight cancelled by airline then insurance company will give non refundable expenses?
comments.author: @SKapoorize

comments.text: You need to check the fine print or call their call centre and get these queries cleared.
comments.author: @heenajshah

comments.text: We have planned switzeland taking emirates on 28 apr. Keeping fingers crossed 🤞  we have refundable tickets. Flight is operating. Madam, while finalizing itenarary we did take some tips from your related videos.
comments.author: @hirenvora-xt7xp

comments.text: I hope all goes as planned for your trip. I am so glad tips from my videos are helpful in planning your trip. Happy Travels 😊
comments.author: @heenajshah

comments.text: My trip is scheduled form 4 april to 18 april and my flight was booked via dubai but I cancelled it and booked same schedule flight via Jeddah is it okk??
comments.author: @ashishpanwar2807

comments.text: While flights to major European hubs remain on schedule, routes may be subject to disruption, delay or re-routing. The best thing to do is monitor the airline travel advisory for latest updates.
comments.author: @heenajshah

comments.text: Not sure what to do .. trip planned for Month of May
comments.author: @shreyaraikar4144

comments.text: You can check the airlines travel advisory. Moreover, European destinations are safe, so you should be good to go. Have a good travel insurance covering flight cancellations, delays, etc.
comments.author: @heenajshah
